points.text: Immatics presented Phase 1 data on IMA401, a MAGEA4/8 bispecific T-cell engager, at the 2026 ASCO Annual Meeting with supporting publication in Nature Medicine.
Immunocore reported Q1 2026 net sales of $106.7 million for KIMMTRAK (tebentafusp-tebn), a bispecific T-cell engager, representing a 14% year-over-year increase.

BioNTech and Bristol Myers Squibb presented global data for pumitamig, a PD-L1xVEGF-A bispecific, showing encouraging anti-tumor activity in first-line non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) during the Phase 2/3 ROSETTA Lung-02 trial interim analysis.

Immatics reported clinical activity for IMA401, a MAGEA4/8 TCR bispecific, demonstrating deep and durable responses in head and neck cancer with a 29% confirmed ORR and 64% DCR at the recommended Phase 2 dose.
Immatics is advancing a combination strategy of IMA401 (MAGEA4/8 bispecific) and IMA402 (PRAME bispecific) in lung cancer, with the first cohort now enrolling and topline data expected in 2027.

Genmab A/S presented 23 abstracts at ASCO 2026 and EHA 2026, including 20 evaluating epcoritamab, a subcutaneous T-cell engaging bispecific antibody, across monotherapy, combination, and fixed-duration settings.

Crescent Biopharma announced a trial-in-progress presentation for the ASCEND Phase 1/2 study of CR-001, a PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody, at ASCO 2026, with proof-of-concept data expected in Q1 2027.

Immatics N.V. — IMA401 — MAGEA4/8-positive solid tumors — Presented clinical data on its MAGEA4/8 bispecific T-cell engager at ASCO 2026 with simultaneous publication in Nature Medicine supporting combination development with IMA402 in lung cancer.

BioNTech and Bristol Myers Squibb presented global data for pumitamig, a PD-L1xVEGF-A bispecific antibody, showing encouraging efficacy in first-line non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) from the Phase 2/3 ROSETTA Lung-02 trial.

Regeneron — Linvoseltamab — Relapsed/refractory systemic light chain (AL) amyloidosis — Phase 1/2 trial: 100% objective response rate (20/20 patients) and 90% hematologic complete response rate (18/20) with no dose-limiting toxicities reported at ASCO 2026.

Akeso Therapeutics and Summit Therapeutics — ivonescimab — squamous non-small cell lung cancer — Phase 3 trial data presented at ASCO 2026 showed 34% reduction in risk of death vs standard of care; described as a 'two drugs in one' bispecific antibody.
BioNTech and Pfizer presented bispecific antibody data at the ASCO 2026 annual meeting.

Peter Forsberg (Colorado Blood Cancer Institute) presented phase 2 OPTec/OPTal trial data showing prophylactic tocilizumab enabled outpatient bispecific antibody administration for relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma with zero grade 3+ CRS or ICANS events.

Janssen (teclistamab-cqyv/Tecvayli) and Janssen (talquetamab-tgvs/Talvey) bispecific antibodies demonstrated 68.9% and 71.4% overall response rates respectively in the OPTec/OPTal trial when combined with prophylactic tocilizumab.

Janssen/Johnson & Johnson — Teclistamab (Tecvayli) — Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma (RRMM) — Phase 3 MajesTEC-9 trial: HR 0.29 for PFS (71% risk reduction), median PFS not reached vs 8.2 months; 18-month OS rate 79.2% vs 68.6% despite >66% of control arm receiving subsequent bispecific antibody or CAR-T therapy.

Multiple Myeloma — Outpatient Bispecific Antibody Administration — Clinical Trend — Tocilizumab identified as supportive agent enabling outpatient administration of bispecific antibodies in multiple myeloma settings.

Community oncology practices are transitioning from academic tertiary centers to outpatient settings for bispecific T-cell engagers (BiTEs) due to evolving safety protocols for cytokine release syndrome (CRS) and immune effector cell-associated neurotoxicity syndrome (ICANS).

Operational models for bispecific antibody administration vary from purely community-based outpatient clinics to hybrid academic-community systems, with patient volumes ranging from fewer than 5 to 50 weekly per site.
Tocilizumab is utilized to support the safe outpatient administration of bispecific antibodies in multiple myeloma patients, enabling step-up dosing outside of inpatient settings.

Akeso Therapeutics and Summit Therapeutics — ivonescimab — squamous non-small cell lung cancer — Phase 3 trial data presented at ASCO 2026 showed 34% reduction in risk of death vs standard of care; described as 'two drugs in one' bispecific antibody.
Zanidatamab-hrii (Ziihera) — Bispecific antibody — Upper GI cancers — Anticipated regulatory approval expected before expansion into colorectal cancer landscape per ASCO 2026 preview.
Tocilizumab — Supportive care for bispecific antibody therapy — Multiple Myeloma — Enables outpatient administration of bispecific antibodies per ASCO 2026 presentation trends.
Immatics — IMA401 — MAGEA4/8-positive solid tumors — Presented Phase 1 clinical data and Nature Medicine publication at 2026 ASCO supporting IMA401/IMA402 combination development in lung cancer.
Immunocore — Brenetafusp — Heavily pretreated advanced melanoma — Presented updated Phase 1 data at 2026 ASCO for the bispecific T-cell engager.

BioNTech / Bristol Myers Squibb — Pumitamig (BNT327/BMS986545) — First-line Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer — Phase 2/3 ROSETTA Lung-02 interim analysis showed encouraging anti-tumor activity for the PD-L1xVEGF-A bispecific in combination with chemotherapy.

Immatics — IMA401 — Head and Neck Cancer — Phase 1 trial at recommended Phase 2 dose (RP2D) achieved 29% confirmed ORR (4/14), 64% DCR (9/14), and median duration of response of 8.8 months with or without pembrolizumab.
Immatics — IMA401/IMA402 Combination — Lung Cancer — Phase 1 combination cohort of MAGEA4/8 and PRAME bispecifics now enrolling at multiple sites; first data expected in 2027.
Genmab A/S — Epcoritamab — Relapsed/Refractory Large B-Cell Lymphoma — Phase 3 EPCORE DLBCL-1 trial full results presented comparing monotherapy to investigator's choice chemotherapy.
Genmab A/S — Epcoritamab — Relapsed/Refractory Follicular Lymphoma — Phase 3 EPCORE FL-1 trial data presented evaluating combination with rituximab and lenalidomide (R2) versus R2 alone.

Crescent Biopharma — CR-001 — NSCLC, GI, and Gynecological Cancers — ASCEND Phase 1/2 global trial ongoing with enrollment up to 290 patients across US, Europe, and Asia Pacific; primary endpoint safety/tolerability.

Immatics — IMA401 — MAGEA4/8-positive solid tumors — MAGEA4/8 bispecific T-cell engager data presented at ASCO 2026; Nature Medicine publication supports IMA401/IMA402 combination strategy in lung cancer.

Regeneron — Linvoseltamab — Relapsed/refractory systemic light chain (AL) amyloidosis — Bispecific antibody demonstrated rapid clinical activity in Phase 1/2 trial; 18 of 20 patients achieved hematologic complete response with manageable safety profile.

Akeso Therapeutics / Summit Therapeutics — ivonescimab — squamous non-small cell lung cancer — ASCO 2026 presentation of Phase 3 data demonstrating 34% mortality risk reduction vs standard therapy; drug classified as a bispecific antibody combining two best-selling cancer medicine activities.
BioNTech / Pfizer — Bispecific Antibodies — Oncology — Presented clinical data at ASCO 2026 meeting.

Janssen — Teclistamab-cqyv (Tecvayli) — Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma — Phase 2 OPTec/OPTal trial: 68.9% ORR with prophylactic tocilizumab; 0 grade 3+ CRS/ICANS; supports community-based outpatient administration.

Janssen — Talquetamab-tgvs (Talvey) — Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma — Phase 2 OPTec/OPTal trial: 71.4% ORR with prophylactic tocilizumab; 0 grade 3+ CRS/ICANS; supports community-based outpatient administration.
Janssen/Johnson & Johnson — Teclistamab (Tecvayli) — RRMM — Phase 3 MajesTEC-9: ORR 84.5% vs 54.2% (PVd/Kd); CR or better 65.9% vs 16.8%; median DOR not estimable vs 13.4 months.
Tocilizumab — Bispecific Antibody Support — Multiple Myeloma — Clinical data presented at ASCO 2026 supports use of tocilizumab to facilitate outpatient bispecific antibody administration.

CancerNetwork Roundtable — Bispecific Antibodies — Community Oncology — Established actionable blueprints for community practice readiness, safety infrastructure, and programmatic sustainability for T-cell engagers.

University of Kansas Health System — Bispecific Antibodies — Hematologic Malignancies — Implemented centralized institutional algorithms and order sets to standardize bispecific therapy administration across community satellite clinics.

Akeso Therapeutics / Summit Therapeutics — ivonescimab — squamous non-small cell lung cancer — ASCO 2026 presentation: 34% reduction in death risk vs standard treatment; data published in The Lancet; drug characterized as bispecific combining two best-selling cancer medicine activities.
Zanidatamab-hrii — Ziihera — Upper GI cancers — Bispecific antibody anticipated for regulatory approval in 2026 prior to colorectal cancer indication expansion.
Immatics — Bispecific T-cell engager development — IMA401 (MAGEA4/8) + IMA402 pipeline — Advancing combination therapy in lung cancer with Phase 1 data presented at ASCO 2026.
Immunocore — Commercialization of bispecific T-cell engagers — KIMMTRAK (tebentafusp-tebn) + Brenetafusp pipeline — Q1 2026 revenue $106.7M (+14% YoY); $845M cash position as of March 31, 2026.

BioNTech — Late-stage oncology pipeline expansion — Pumitamig (PD-L1xVEGF-A bispecific) + Gotistobart — Advancing 25+ Phase 2/3 trials including 13 pivotal studies; presenting ROSETTA Lung-02 data at ASCO 2026.

Immatics — Precision targeting of PRAME and MAGEA4/8 — IMA401 (MAGEA4/8 TCR bispecific) + IMA402 (PRAME bispecific) — Phase 1 data supports broad combinability; IMA401/IMA402 combination enrolling for lung cancer indications.
Genmab A/S — Bispecific antibody portfolio expansion — Epcoritamab (T-cell engaging bispecific) — 20 abstracts presented at ASCO/EHA 2026 covering multiple B-cell malignancy settings.
Crescent Biopharma — Next-generation immuno-oncology backbone — CR-001 (PD-1 x VEGF bispecific) — Phase 1/2 ASCEND trial enrolling 290 patients; initial data readout anticipated Q1 2027.

Immatics N.V. — Precision targeting of PRAME and MAGEA4/8 — IMA203CD8 (PRAME TCR T-cell therapy) + IMA401 (MAGEA4/8 bispecific) — Phase 1 data presented at ASCO 2026; RP2D for IMA203CD8 expected in 2026.

BioNTech — Advancing late-stage oncology pipeline with bispecific and ADC modalities — Pumitamig (PD-L1xVEGF-A bispecific) + Gotistobart — 25+ Phase 2/3 trials ongoing including 13 pivotal trials; ROSETTA Lung-02 data presented at ASCO 2026.

Bristol Myers Squibb — Co-development of bispecific immunomodulators — Pumitamig (BMS986545) — Collaborating with BioNTech on PD-L1xVEGF-A bispecific for NSCLC; Phase 2/3 ROSETTA Lung-02 trial ongoing.

Regeneron — Advancing bispecific antibody pipeline for plasma cell disorders — Linvoseltamab (Lynozyfic) — Phase 1/2 data in AL amyloidosis shows 100% ORR and 90% CR; positioned to address unmet need in relapsed/refractory setting where no approved therapies exist.

Summit Therapeutics — Commercialization of bispecific antibody ivonescimab outside China — ivonescimab — Phase 3 data presented at ASCO 2026 showing 34% reduction in death risk for squamous NSCLC; co-CEO Robert Duggan cited 'very valuable business' potential.

Akeso Therapeutics — Development of bispecific antibody ivonescimab in China — ivonescimab — Conducted Phase 3 trial entirely in China; data presented at ASCO 2026 showing 34% mortality risk reduction vs standard of care for squamous NSCLC.
BioNTech — Bispecific antibody development — Oncology pipeline — Presented data at ASCO 2026.
Pfizer — Bispecific antibody development — Oncology pipeline — Presented data at ASCO 2026.

Janssen — Outpatient bispecific antibody commercialization strategy — Teclistamab-cqyv (Tecvayli) + Talquetamab-tgvs (Talvey) — Phase 2 OPTec/OPTal data supports shift to community-based administration using prophylactic tocilizumab to mitigate CRS.

Janssen/Johnson & Johnson — Bispecific antibody commercialization in myeloma — Teclistamab (Tecvayli) — Phase 3 MajesTEC-9 met primary endpoint with 71% PFS risk reduction; control arm crossover included bispecific antibody usage.

Summit Therapeutics — Commercialization of bispecific antibody ivonescimab outside China — ivonescimab — Phase 3 data presented at ASCO 2026 showing 34% mortality reduction in squamous NSCLC; co-CEO Robert Duggan cited 'very valuable business' potential.

Akeso Therapeutics — Development of bispecific antibody ivonescimab in China — ivonescimab — Conducted Phase 3 trial entirely in China; results presented at ASCO 2026; no comment provided on data implications.
Jazz Pharmaceuticals (implied via Ziihera) — Bispecific antibody commercialization — Zanidatamab-hrii — Anticipated approval in upper GI cancers before CRC expansion.

IMA401 — Mechanism: MAGEA4/8 bispecific T-cell engager — Phase: 1 — Population: MAGEA4/8-positive solid tumors (lung cancer focus) — Endpoints: Clinical activity data presented at ASCO 2026 with concurrent Nature Medicine publication.
Brenetafusp — Mechanism: Bispecific T-cell engager — Phase: 1 — Population: Heavily pretreated advanced melanoma — Endpoints: Updated clinical activity data presented at ASCO 2026.

Pumitamig — Mechanism: PD-L1 checkpoint inhibition and VEGF-A neutralization (bispecific) — Phase: 2/3 (ROSETTA Lung-02) — Population: First-line NSCLC — Comparator: Chemotherapy combination — Results: Encouraging anti-tumor activity in interim analysis.

IMA401 — Mechanism: TCR bispecific targeting MAGEA4/8 — Phase: 1 — Population: Heavily pretreated solid tumors (Head and Neck, Lung) — Comparator: None (monotherapy or with pembrolizumab) — Results: 29% ORR, 64% DCR, 8.8 months mDOR in Head and Neck cancer; favorable tolerability at RP2D.

Epcoritamab — Mechanism: T-cell engaging bispecific antibody — Phase: 3 — Population: Relapsed/Refractory Large B-Cell Lymphoma — Comparator: Investigator's choice chemotherapy — Status: Full results from EPCORE DLBCL-1 presented at ASCO 2026.

CR-001 — Mechanism: PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody — Phase: 1/2 — Population: Solid tumors (NSCLC, GI, Gyn) — Comparator: None specified (monotherapy/combination) — Endpoints: Safety, PK/PD, ORR, DoR, PFS, OS; data expected Q1 2027.

IMA401 — Mechanism: MAGEA4/8 bispecific T-cell engager — Phase: 1 — Population: MAGEA4/8-positive solid tumors — Comparator: Not specified — Status: Clinical data presented at ASCO 2026; combination with IMA402 under investigation for lung cancer.

Pumitamig — Mechanism: Bispecific immunomodulator combining PD-L1 checkpoint inhibition and VEGF-A neutralization — Phase: 2/3 (ROSETTA Lung-02) — Population: First-line non-small cell lung cancer — Comparator: Chemotherapy combination — Results: Interim analysis showed encouraging anti-tumor activity.

Linvoseltamab — Mechanism: Bispecific antibody targeting CD38 and BCMA (implied by context of AL amyloidosis/myeloma activity) — Phase: 1/2 — Population: Relapsed/refractory systemic light chain (AL) amyloidosis — Comparator: None (single-arm) — Endpoints/Results: 100% objective response rate, 90% hematologic complete response rate, no dose-limiting toxicities.

ivonescimab — Mechanism: Bispecific antibody combining two best-selling cancer medicine activities — Phase: 3 — Population: Squamous non-small cell lung cancer — Comparator: Standard of care — Results: 34% reduction in risk of death; data presented at ASCO 2026 and published in The Lancet.
BioNTech/Pfizer bispecifics — Mechanism: Bispecific antibody — Phase: Not specified — Population: Oncology patients — Comparator: Not specified — Results: Data presented at ASCO 2026.

Teclistamab-cqyv — Mechanism: BCMAxCD3 bispecific T-cell engager — Phase: 2 (OPTec/OPTal) — Population: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma — Comparator: Historical controls (implied) — Endpoints/Results: 68.9% ORR, 17.8% CR, 0 grade 3+ CRS/ICANS with prophylactic tocilizumab.

Talquetamab-tgvs — Mechanism: GPRC5DxCD3 bispecific T-cell engager — Phase: 2 (OPTec/OPTal) — Population: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma — Comparator: Historical controls (implied) — Endpoints/Results: 71.4% ORR, 14.3% CR, 0 grade 3+ CRS/ICANS with prophylactic tocilizumab.

Teclistamab — Mechanism: BCMAxCD3 bispecific T-cell engager — Phase: 3 (MajesTEC-9) — Population: RRMM (1-3 prior lines) — Comparator: PVd or Kd — Results: HR 0.29 for PFS, HR 0.60 for OS; 18-month PFS 69.8% vs 26.9%.

Tocilizumab — Mechanism: IL-6 receptor antagonist — Phase: Clinical practice support — Population: Patients receiving bispecific antibodies — Comparator: N/A — Results: Enables outpatient administration of bispecific antibody therapies in multiple myeloma.

Bispecific T-cell engagers (BiTEs) — Mechanism: T-cell redirection to tumor cells — Phase: Commercial/Post-marketing — Population: Hematologic malignancies and solid tumors — Safety Profile: Historically constrained by CRS and ICANS, now managed via outpatient step-up dosing protocols.

Tocilizumab — Mechanism: IL-6 receptor antagonist — Phase: Commercial — Population: Multiple Myeloma patients on bispecific therapy — Comparator: None stated — Outcome: Enables outpatient bispecific antibody use by mitigating cytokine release syndrome.

ivonescimab — Mechanism: Bispecific antibody combining two best-selling cancer medicine activities — Phase: 3 — Population: Squamous non-small cell lung cancer patients — Comparator: Standard treatment — Results: 34% reduction in risk of death; data presented at ASCO 2026 and published in The Lancet.

Zanidatamab-hrii — Mechanism: Bispecific antibody targeting HER2 — Phase: Pre-approval/Regulatory review — Population: Upper GI cancers — Comparator: N/A — Status: Anticipated approval before CRC expansion.

Bispecific Antibodies — Mechanism: Dual-target engagement (context: Multiple Myeloma) — Phase: Clinical use — Population: Multiple Myeloma patients — Comparator: N/A — Outcome: Outpatient administration enabled via Tocilizumab support.
Immunocore — Commercial performance of bispecific product KIMMTRAK — Q1 2026 net sales $106.7M (+14% YoY); US sales $67.4M, Europe $34.4M, International $4.8M.

Summit Therapeutics — Commercialization strategy for bispecific ivonescimab — ivonescimab — Co-CEO Robert Duggan stated data confirms 'very valuable business' with product in early stages; development outside China led by Summit.
